search_queries_used: site:dha.gov.ae pediatric abdominal pain clinical guideline
PIC endorsed acute abdominal pain children guideline
candidate_sources_rejected: NICE gastro-oesophageal reflux disease in children guideline
Queensland chronic and recurrent abdominal pain referral criteria
rejection_reasons: The DHA source is gastroenteritis-specific rather than an exact abdominal-pain workflow source.
The NICE source is reflux-specific and does not cover undifferentiated pediatric abdominal pain.
The Queensland referral criteria are chronic referral criteria and narrower than the selected acute presentation guideline.
population_applicability: Partial: exact source covers acute abdominal pain in children and adolescents; the baseline includes chronic, recurrent, and unspecified-duration presentations.
setting_applicability: Partial: the source is hospital and emergency focused, while the baseline is a general outpatient documentation workflow.
UAE_applicability: No direct UAE applicability is established; this Australian PIC-endorsed source requires UAE clinician review before application.
recency_verification: Official RCH/PIC guideline page opened on 2026-07-11; page states last updated April 2024.
superseded_check: Official guideline page checked on 2026-07-11; no newer issue than the stated April 2024 update was identified.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Directly supports acute pediatric abdominal-pain presentation terminology and the need to assess for urgent causes.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Defines acute pediatric abdominal pain as a common nonspecific presentation requiring assessment for urgent causes and red flags.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Directly supports mapped pain, systemic, stool, vomiting, urinary, diet, and infant-history domains.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Specifies pain characteristics, systemic symptoms, diet, stool, vomiting, urinary, adolescent, psychosocial, and infant presentation history.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Directly supports mapped appearance, vital signs, hydration, abdominal examination, tenderness, guarding, rigidity, and distension documentation.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Specifies appearance and movement, vital signs, hydration, abdominal inspection, tenderness, guarding, rigidity, masses, and serial examination.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Directly supports mapped urinalysis and clinician-selected investigation documentation while noting that most children need none.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: States that most children need no investigations and lists targeted urine, blood, and imaging investigations selected by the likely cause.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Directly supports mapped severe-pain, peritonism, bilious-vomiting, follow-up, and parent safety-net documentation.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Lists severe pain, peritonism, bilious vomiting, suspected surgical disease, admission, follow-up, and parent advice contexts.

unresolved_source_gaps: No exact UAE pediatric abdominal-pain guideline was identified in the reasonable authoritative search.
Chronic, recurrent, functional, and constipation-labelled content is not fully covered by the acute source.
The selected source is hospital/emergency and Australia-specific rather than UAE outpatient guidance.
Dietary, hydration, laxative, and fixed follow-up content remains unsupported except for documented safety-net and follow-up decisions.
Qualified clinician review has not been completed.
